records.ety: [L. temporalis, fr. tempora the temples: cf. F. temporal. See Temple a part of the head.]

defs: Of or pertaining to the temple or temples; as, the temporal bone; a temporal artery.

records.ety: [L. temporalis, fr. tempus, temporis, time, portion of time, the fitting or appointed time: cf. F. temporel. Cf. Contemporaneous, Extempore, Temper, v. t., Tempest, Temple a part of the head, Tense, n., Thing.]

defs: Of or pertaining to time, that is, to the present life, or this world; secular, as distinguished from sacred or eternal.
quotes: The things which are seen are temporal, but the things which are not seen are eternal.
Is this an hour for temporal affairs?

defs: Civil or political, as distinguished from ecclesiastical; as, temporal power; temporal courts.
Anything temporal or secular; a temporality; -- used chiefly in the plural.
quotes: He assigns supremacy to the pope in spirituals, and to the emperor or temporals.
